sector: Industrials / Nuclear (defense + commercial nuclear components, fuel & services, medical isotopes)
bull_thesis: BWXT is the closest thing to a sole-source toll on the U.S. nuclear renaissance, and it now sits at the intersection of three converging secular waves: (1) the AI-datacenter power crunch driving SMR/microreactor demand, (2) HALEU/HEU enrichment reshoring (Tennessee NRC engagement), and (3) a defense spine that is effectively un-disruptable. The government franchise — the only U.S. manufacturer of naval nuclear reactors for Virginia/Columbia submarines and Ford carriers — is a regulatory-license + security-clearance monopoly that produced 20.4% segment EBITDA margins and grew 8% in FY25 with multi-decade visibility (long-lead Columbia/Virginia work, the next Ford ship-set). On top of that annuity, commercial nuclear is inflecting hard: +63% FY25 to $853M, +121% in Q1-26, with management raising commercial growth guidance to ~30%. The proof is the backlog: $8.65-8.7B at Q1-26, +77% YoY and +19% sequentially — ~2.3x annual revenue, the strongest visibility in the basket. Management is funding a Made-in-USA commercial-nuclear footprint (PCG acquisition + contemplated Mount Vernon greenfield) precisely as customers demand localized capacity across the U.S., Canada and Europe. Beat-and-raise cadence is intact (Q1-26 EPS $1.12, +22%, beat by ~$0.20); FY26 guidance was lifted across revenue (>$3.75B), EBITDA ($650-665M), EPS ($4.60-4.75) and FCF ($315-330M) after just one quarter. Picks-and-shovels exposure to AI-power and SMR with a defense floor underneath — the rare scale-out name whose demand does NOT collapse if hyperscaler capex pauses, because the Navy keeps paying. Conditions: commercial ramp scales toward government-like margins; greenfield/PCG capex converts to FCF; NRC enrichment milestones advance.
bear_thesis: You are paying a wide-moat multiple for a ~10.5%-ROIC compounder, and the AI/SMR/HALEU optionality is already heavily pre-paid. At ~$205, ~$18.7B market cap and ~$19.5B EV, BWXT trades at ~55x trailing / ~44x forward earnings and ~30-33x forward EV/EBITDA — a ~2.4x premium to defense peers — on a business whose ROIC is only ~10.5% and whose FCF yield is ~1.6%. The headline growth flatters the underlying engine: of the 26% Q1-26 revenue growth, organic was only ~11%; the rest is acquired (Kinectrics, A.O.T., now PCG). Commercial nuclear, the re-rating driver, is still a single-digit-to-low-teens share of EBITDA and carries a 12.9% margin that DILUTES the 20.4% government margin as it grows, structurally capping blended-margin expansion. The capital story has quietly degraded from a net-cash buyback compounder (only ~$20M repurchased in 2025, 0.36% yield) to a leverage-up M&A/greenfield spender: ~$1.5B net debt and rising, ~2.3x EBITDA, with Mount Vernon and PCG consuming cash before they generate it. The load-bearing commercial catalysts are years out and regulatory-gated: the Tennessee HEU/HALEU enrichment facility is NRC-process-dependent, SMR component volumes depend on third-party reactor deployments that keep slipping, and medical isotopes remain a slow-burn option. The defense base is real and durable but grows GDP-plus, not 30% — it cannot carry a 55x multiple alone. Any commercial-ramp disappointment, margin-dilution surprise, or NRC delay de-rates a stock priced for near-flawless execution. Confirmation: organic growth decelerating below ~10%, commercial EBITDA margin failing to climb, FCF guidance cut on capex, or an enrichment/NRC timeline push-out.
sop_bull: $245-255/sh. Government Operations: ~$479M FY25 adj EBITDA growing ~8%, value at 16x (defense-aerospace, sole-source naval premium) = ~$7.7B EV. Commercial Operations: ~$110M FY25 adj EBITDA growing 30-50% with SMR/HALEU/medical optionality, value at 28-32x forward as a high-growth nuclear-reshoring pure-play = ~$3.5-4.0B EV. Sum operating EV ~$11.2-11.7B... but the bull case is a multiple/optionality story: applying a 33x EV/EBITDA on ~$655M FY26 guided adj EBITDA = ~$21.6B EV, less ~$1.5B net debt = ~$20.1B equity / ~91M sh ~ $220, plus HALEU enrichment + space-microreactor + medical-isotope call-options worth ~$25-35/sh in a successful commercial-ramp scenario => ~$245-255. Backlog ($8.65B) underwrites the revenue line and de-risks the EBITDA bridge.
sop_bear: $140-160/sh. Re-rate to a defense-quality multiple. Government Operations ~$479M EBITDA at 14x = ~$6.7B EV; Commercial ~$110M EBITDA at 16x (margin-dilutive, capex-heavy, execution-gated) = ~$1.8B EV; total operating EV ~$8.5B less ~$1.5B net debt = ~$7.0B equity. On a blended-company basis: ~$655M FY26 EBITDA at 18-20x EV/EBITDA (still a premium to defense peers for the moat) = ~$11.8-13.1B EV, less ~$1.5B net debt = ~$10.3-11.6B equity / ~91M sh = ~$113-128; a less harsh 22x = ~$143. Bear midpoint ~$150 — i.e. the AI/SMR premium fully unwinds and you hold a quality defense compounder at ~30x earnings.
sop_breakdown: Whole-company base case: FY26 guided adj EBITDA ~$655M at a 26-28x EV/EBITDA blended multiple — a justified premium to defense peers (~13-15x) for the WIDE sole-source naval moat + genuine commercial-nuclear growth, but a discount to the current ~30-33x because ROIC is only ~10.5% and the commercial optionality is lightly de-risked. 27x x $655M = ~$17.7B EV, less ~$1.5B net debt = ~$16.2B equity / ~91M sh ~ $178; add ~$12-18/sh for HALEU/SMR/medical optionality not yet in EBITDA = ~$190-196. Cross-check on earnings: ~$4.68 FY26 EPS x 40-42x (premium-but-not-peak) = ~$187-197. Segment cross-check: Gov ~$7.2B EV (15x) + Commercial ~$2.6B EV (24x) + ~$0.4B options - $1.5B net debt = ~$8.7B... which is well below the market and the multiple view; the gap IS the priced-in optionality. Base fair ~$192, i.e. modestly below the ~$205 spot.
risk_reward: Unfavorable-to-neutral. Spot ~$205. Upside-to-fair ~$192 base is NEGATIVE (~-6%); upside to the bull ~$250 is +22%; downside to the bear ~$150 is -27%. Risk/reward ~0.8:1 against (downside-to-break exceeds upside-to-fair-value). Even on the bull/bear span the asymmetry is roughly symmetric (+22% / -27%), and the base case sits slightly below spot — you are buying a strengthening business at a price that already discounts a successful commercial ramp.
catalyst_status: SOFT_EXTENDED. The load-bearing commercial catalysts are real but non-binding, third-party-dependent and serially-extending: (1) Tennessee HEU/HALEU enrichment facility is at the NRC-engagement stage (multi-year licensing, no fixed in-service date) — confirmed in Q1-26 call (05/04/2026); (2) SMR/microreactor component volumes depend on third-party reactor deployments (Oklo, X-energy et al.) that keep slipping; (3) Mount Vernon greenfield is 'being evaluated,' not committed. The HARD, already-fired catalysts (Q1-26 beat-and-raise, $8.65B backlog print, PCG acquisition announced) are spent — the stock re-rated to 55x ON them. So the live catalysts are SOFT_EXTENDED (regulatory/third-party, no binding date), while the re-rating drivers are FIRED. No PENDING_HARD dated event with real asymmetry in the next 3-9 months.
peer_comps_note: BWXT sits in scale-out Basket E (POWER-GEN) as a picks-and-shovels nuclear toll. TRUE peers split across two cohorts. (1) Nuclear-fuel/enrichment pure-plays: Centrus Energy (LEU) — Q1-26 adj EPS $1.05, $900M HALEU award, raised rev guide $450-500M, signed non-binding LOI w/ Oklo 06/18/2026 for 2029 HALEU; far smaller, lumpier, more direct HALEU leverage but no defense annuity. Cameco (CCJ) — uranium miner, 2026 EPS +17.5% / 2027 +58.7% (Zacks), upstream-fuel exposure, trades at a high uranium-cycle multiple. (2) Defense/nuclear-component primes: Curtiss-Wright (CW, naval/defense nuclear components, reported Q1-26) — closest defense-multiple anchor at ~25-28x earnings; mainline A&D primes (LMT/GD/HII) trade ~15-18x. On the comp grid BWXT ranks: VALUATION — most expensive of the defense cohort (~44x fwd P/E, ~30-33x fwd EV/EBITDA, a ~2.4x premium to defense peers); cheaper than the SMR-developer cohort (Oklo/SMR, pre-earnings, no comparable multiple) but far more profitable. GROWTH — top-quartile among profitable nuclear names (26% rev, +77% backlog) but below pure-play enrichment optionality (LEU). MARGINS — best-in-class government segment (20.4% EBITDA) vs Centrus/commercial peers; blended diluting toward commercial. RETURNS — ~10.5% ROIC is MIDDLE of the pack, below high-return defense electronics (CW ~13-15%), undermining the premium multiple. AI-CAPEX EXPOSURE — high-quality and DEFENSE-FLOORED: unlike pure-SMR plays whose entire thesis is hyperscaler power, BWXT's Navy spine keeps paying if AI-capex pauses — the most durable demand profile in the basket, which partly justifies a premium but not 2x the cohort. Net: BWXT is the highest-quality, most-de-risked name in POWER-GEN, priced accordingly — the premium is earned on moat/durability but stretched on returns/valuation.
role_in_scaleout: BWXT is the defense-floored nuclear toll of the build-out: it supplies the reactor components, special materials, HALEU/enrichment reshoring and SMR/microreactor manufacturing that the AI-power/SMR layer must pass through — and because its government spine (sole-source U.S. naval reactors) keeps paying regardless of hyperscaler capex, its demand is the most durable and least AI-capex-dependent in the basket; the commercial-nuclear upside is real but multi-year and regulatory-gated.
consensus_delta: There is little exploitable consensus delta here — the street is NOT looking the wrong way; it has fully embraced BWXT as 'the nuclear company' and re-rated it to ~55x trailing / ~44x forward earnings with a ~$238 average target (+19%). The expectations-arbitrage cuts AGAINST a long: consensus already extrapolates the ~30% commercial growth and treats the HALEU/SMR optionality as near-certain, when (a) organic growth is only ~11% (rest is M&A), (b) commercial's 12.9% EBITDA margin dilutes the 20.4% government margin so blended-margin expansion is structurally capped, and (c) the enrichment/SMR catalysts are NRC- and third-party-gated with no binding dates. The mispricing is on the QUALITY-OF-GROWTH and RETURNS axis: the multiple implies a returns profile (mid-teens+ ROIC, accelerating organic) that a ~10.5% ROIC, M&A-assisted grower does not deliver. The bullish street assumption most likely to be wrong: that commercial scales to government-like profitability quickly — the mix shift says the opposite near-term.
valley_of_death: No solvency or cash-burn cliff — the Navy backlog and ~$315-330M guided FCF insulate the balance sheet; ~$1.5B net debt at ~2.3x EBITDA is investment-grade-comfortable. The 3-9 month risk is MULTIPLE, not fundamental: (1) capex/greenfield cash drag — Mount Vernon evaluation + PCG integration consume cash before generating it, and any FCF-guidance trim on capex would hit a stock priced at a ~1.6% FCF yield hard; (2) a single soft commercial quarter or an NRC/enrichment timeline push-out would puncture the 'accelerating commercial' narrative that justifies the premium; (3) basket-level AI-capex de-grossing risk — the regime doc flags AI-capex as ~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth and the dominant shared factor; a hyperscaler capex scare would de-rate the entire POWER-GEN cohort (Oklo/SMR/LEU/BWXT) together even though BWXT's Navy spine is orthogonal. BWXT would fall LESS than the pure-plays but would not be spared a cohort de-rate from a 55x starting point.
positioning_washout: No yield-base washout risk — the 0.36% dividend means there is no income-holder cohort to force-sell, and only ~$20M of buybacks means no support-bid either. The shareholder base is growth/thematic momentum money that bought the nuclear-renaissance re-rating; that is the vulnerability — momentum ownership at 55x unwinds violently on any growth-narrative crack or a broad AI-capex de-grossing, with no value/income floor underneath to absorb it. There is no mechanical forced-seller catalyst to exploit on the LONG side here; if anything the washout risk is asymmetric to the downside.
forcing_function: No clean near-term forcing function that re-rates UP — the hard catalysts (Q1-26 beat-and-raise 05/04/2026; $8.65B backlog print; PCG announcement) already FIRED and the re-rating is spent. The next scheduled events are Q2-26 earnings (~early Aug 2026, another beat-and-raise is largely expected/priced) and incremental, undated NRC engagement on the Tennessee enrichment facility plus a Mount Vernon go/no-go decision — none binding, none dated. The realistic forcing function is to the DOWNSIDE: a soft commercial quarter, a capex-driven FCF trim, an NRC/SMR timeline slip, or a cohort-wide AI-capex de-grossing that resets the 55x multiple.
moderator_conclusion: BWXT is the highest-quality, most-durable name in scale-out Basket E — a genuine WIDE-moat sole-source naval-nuclear monopoly with a real, strengthening commercial-nuclear ramp and best-in-class $8.65B backlog (+77% YoY). The INTERROGATOR is right: trajectory STRENGTHENING, moat WIDE/STABLE, no secular threat — it is a beneficiary, not a victim, of the AI-power/SMR/reshoring waves. But the CRO mandate is timing, saturation and margin-of-safety, and on all three the name fails as a fresh long. APPLYING THE REGIME (2026-06-05 read): the doc rewards HARD-DATED catalysts inside the favorable window and PENALIZES already-fired/already-ran catalysts, rate-rescue theses, and names whose thesis hinges on the SAME shared macro factor (AI-capex durability) as the rest of the basket. BWXT's re-rating catalysts are FIRED (the beat-and-raise and backlog print already drove it to 55x); the LIVE catalysts (HALEU/NRC, SMR, Mount Vernon) are SOFT_EXTENDED — non-binding, third-party/regulatory-gated, no dates; and the thesis shares the basket's single dominant resolution driver (AI-capex), so it FAILS the §1.7 book-independence test — it would de-rate alongside Oklo/SMR/LEU in any AI-capex scare. Base-case SoP fair value ~$192 sits modestly BELOW the ~$205 spot; risk/reward is ~0.8:1 against (downside-to-bear ~-27% vs upside-to-fair ~-6%, upside-to-bull +22%). The ~2.4x premium to defense peers is earned on durability but stretched against a ~10.5% ROIC and ~1.6% FCF yield. VERDICT C / conviction 2: the business is excellent but the stock is saturated, the re-rating is spent, and there is no asymmetric hard catalyst in-window — this is a 'watch for a flush' name, not an entry. The specific event to wait for: a broad AI-capex de-grossing or a single soft commercial quarter that resets the multiple toward the high-30s P/E, where the WIDE moat + secular tailwind would make it a high-conviction durable compounder. VALUE_CONVICTION 3: stripped of catalyst/regime, the value case is a quality WIDE-moat franchise trading modestly above a defensible intrinsic — not cheap (no margin of safety at spot), not a trap (durable, growing, no secular erosion) — a fair price for a great business, capped at 3 by the absence of any discount to fair value.
interrogator_dossier:
# BWX Technologies (BWXT) — Forensic Dossier

*Interrogator pass, Claude Opus 4.8. Scale-out Basket E (POWER-GEN). Data: Q1-2026 release + call (05/04/2026), FY2025 results (02/23/2026), 10-Q (3/31/2026), live quote 06/21/2026. No data bundled — all web-sourced.*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

BWXT's story has moved through three distinct phases. For most of the last decade it was framed as a **defense monopoly with an annuity** — the sole manufacturer of naval nuclear reactors for the U.S. Navy's Virginia/Columbia submarines and Ford carriers, a regulated-margin, low-beta, GDP-plus compounder. Over 2024–2025 management widened the lens to **"the nuclear company"**, deliberately attaching the franchise to three secular waves: (1) the AI-datacenter power crunch and the SMR/microreactor build-out, (2) HALEU/enrichment domestic-supply reshoring, and (3) medical isotopes. By Q1-2026 the narrative has tilted decisively **commercial-first**: management now leads with commercial nuclear, raised commercial growth guidance to ~30% (from "high-teens"), and is funding a U.S. commercial-nuclear manufacturing footprint via the Precision Components Group (PCG) acquisition plus a contemplated greenfield at Mount Vernon, Indiana. The government franchise is now positioned as the durable cash spine that funds the commercial option. Growth drivers ADDED across the arc: SMR components, HEU/HALEU enrichment (Tennessee NRC engagement), space microreactors, localized "Made-in-USA" capacity. Nothing material has been quietly dropped — medical isotopes (Tc-99m / actinium) remain a slow-burn call-option.

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

| Mgmt claim | The number | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| "Record demand, accelerating commercial" | Commercial rev +63% FY25 to $853M; +121% in Q1-26; total rev +18% FY25, +26% Q1-26 (11% organic) | **Corroborated.** Acceleration is real and partly M&A-fueled (Kinectrics, A.O.T., now PCG) but organic 11% is genuine. |
| "Record backlog, multi-year visibility" | Backlog $7.3B end-FY25 (+50%) → **$8.65–8.7B Q1-26 (+77% YoY, +19% QoQ)** | **Corroborated, and the single strongest fact.** Backlog ~2.3x annual revenue. |
| "Profitable, expanding" | Op margin ~12.7% FY25; Gov EBITDA margin 20.4%, Commercial 12.9%; adj EBITDA +14% Q1-26 | **Mixed.** Government margin is rich and stable; commercial dilutes the blended margin as mix shifts. Adjusted EBITDA grows but slower than revenue — margin is being traded for growth. |
| "Strong cash generation" | FCF $295M FY25; 2026 guide raised to $315–330M | **Corroborated**, though FCF/share is modest vs an $18.7B cap (~1.6% FCF yield). |
| "Disciplined balance sheet" | ~$2.0B debt, ~$0.51B cash → **~$1.5B net debt**; leverage ~2.3x EBITDA | **Adequate, not pristine.** Leverage rising as it funds M&A + greenfield capex. Investment-grade-like, covered by Navy cash, but no longer a net-cash story. |
| "Returns justify the franchise" | ROIC ~10.5% | **The soft spot.** A ~10.5% ROIC against a 55x P/E is the central tension — quality, not exceptional capital efficiency. The moat is contractual/regulatory, not a returns-on-capital fortress. |

Where the story is **richer than the numbers**: the valuation embeds commercial-nuclear/SMR optionality that is still a single-digit-% of EBITDA. Where it is **poorer**: the government backlog and Navy sole-source position are arguably under-celebrated relative to how durable they are.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

**Rising.** The cadence of guidance raises (FY26 revenue lifted to >$3.75B, EBITDA to $650–665M, EPS to $4.60–4.75, FCF to $315–330M after only Q1) signals offensive posture. Language is investment/growth-oriented ("customers increasingly asking for localized U.S. capacity," "evaluating a larger greenfield"), not defensive cost-language. Management is leaning into capex and M&A rather than guarding margin — a confidence tell. The only defensive note is the careful framing of enrichment/NRC timelines as multi-year regulatory processes.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

**High.** Q1-26 EPS $1.12 beat by ~$0.20 (+22% YoY); FY25 was a record beat-and-raise; the company raised full-year guidance one quarter in. Pattern is **consistent modest sandbagging then raise** — a reliable, conservatively-guided cadence typical of a cost-plus defense base layered with commercial upside. Reliability: **High.**

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

- **Valuation/multiple** — the recurring pushback is not on the business but on 55x trailing / ~44x forward earnings and ~30–33x EV/EBITDA (a ~2.4x premium to defense peers). Management answers with backlog and TAM, not with a returns bridge.
- **Commercial margin dilution** — as commercial (12.9% EBITDA margin) grows faster than government (20.4%), blended margins compress; analysts probe whether commercial scales to government-like profitability.
- **Capex/greenfield cash drag** — Mount Vernon and PCG integration are cash uses before they are cash generators; the FCF-yield gap vs the multiple is the unspoken worry.
- **Enrichment timeline reality** — HEU/HALEU Tennessee facility is NRC-gated and years out; risk of it being priced as near-term.

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

🟢 Backlog $8.65–8.7B, +77% YoY — best-in-class visibility (Q1-26).
🟢 Sole-source Navy nuclear position — regulatory/security-clearance moat, effectively un-disruptable (structural).
🟢 Beat-and-raise cadence intact; guidance lifted after Q1 (Q1-26).
🟢 Genuine secular tailwind: AI-power, SMR, HALEU reshoring all converge on BWXT's competencies.
🔴 ~55x trailing P/E on a ~10.5% ROIC, ~1.6% FCF yield — the AI/SMR optionality is heavily pre-paid by the market.
🔴 Net debt ~$1.5B and rising; M&A + greenfield capex turn a former net-cash compounder leverage-up.
🔴 Commercial growth is partly acquired (Kinectrics/A.O.T./PCG) — organic is a healthier ~11%, so headline 26% overstates underlying momentum.
🔴 Commercial-mix margin dilution structurally caps blended-margin expansion near-term.

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

The terminology shift from "naval nuclear / defense" toward "**the nuclear company**" and "localized U.S. capacity" is a deliberate repositioning to capture the AI-power and reshoring narrative multiple — and it has worked (the stock re-rated to 55x). Capital allocation has quietly pivoted from buybacks/dividend (only ~$20M repurchased in 2025, 0.36% yield) toward **growth M&A and greenfield capex** — management is signaling it sees higher-return reinvestment than returning cash, consistent with the offensive tone but raising the execution bar. The repeated "customers asking for U.S. capacity" framing is positioning BWXT as the reshoring beneficiary of a fragmented commercial-nuclear supply chain.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

**Trajectory: STRENGTHENING.** Backlog, bookings, guidance and tone all point up, with a credible multi-secular demand stack and a genuine sole-source defense spine. The single most important thing the director must weigh before sizing: **how much of the commercial-nuclear / SMR / HALEU optionality is already in the 55x multiple** — the *business* is strengthening, but the *stock* has priced in a large chunk of a still-nascent commercial ramp; you are paying a wide-moat multiple for a ~10.5%-ROIC compounder whose terminal upside is real but lightly de-risked.

**Moat:** regulatory-license + scale-cost + security-clearance intangible. On the **government** side it is **WIDE** (sole U.S. naval-nuclear-reactor source, decades of qualification, classified-facility barriers) and **STABLE** — effectively un-disruptable, the opposite of a secular-decline name. On the **commercial** side the moat is narrower and being built, not defended. Blended: **WIDE but not widening on returns** — ROIC is ~10.5% and roughly flat-to-down as commercial dilutes, so I score MOAT_TREND **STABLE** (not widening — the franchise is durable but capital efficiency is not improving).

**Secular threat: NONE / manageable.** BWXT is a *beneficiary* of the energy-transition + AI-power + reshoring secular waves, not a victim. There is no AI-displacement, disintermediation, or demand-peak risk to its terminal value; the only "secular" risk is the inverse — that the commercial-nuclear renaissance under-delivers vs the priced-in expectation (a valuation risk, not a terminal-erosion risk). Cheapness is irrelevant here — this is the opposite case: a quality franchise at a rich price.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: WIDE | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: none
skeptic_corrections: The debate frames the Tennessee 'HEU/HALEU enrichment' facility as part of the commercial AI-scale-out optionality. Primary sources show it is materially WEAKER and DIFFERENT: the Erwin, TN facility is HEU for the U.S. Navy under a $1.5B NNSA/DOE contract (Sept 2025) — i.e. DEFENSE supply, not commercial HALEU for SMRs/AI-power; BWXT only NOTIFIED the NRC of intent (pre-application) in April 2026, the actual license application is not expected until Q1-2027, and the facility is targeted to be operational only ~2035. So this is not a 3-9 month catalyst and is not AI-scale-out demand at all; it should be removed from the priced-in commercial call-option. This corrects the catalyst toward SOFTER/LATER than 'SOFT_EXTENDED' and slightly reduces the bull's optionality stack. All other load-bearing numbers verify exactly: backlog $8.65B (Gov $6.93B + Commercial $1.72B), bookings $2.25B, Q1-26 rev $860.2M, commercial rev $283.6M (+121%), net debt $1,505.5M ($2,017.9M debt − $512.4M cash), ~91.9M diluted shares, FY26 guide raised (rev >$3.75B, adj EBITDA $650-665M, EPS $4.60-4.75, FCF $315-330M). Spot ~$205-207, ~$19B cap, ~43x fwd P/E, avg target ~$238 (+17%) — confirms a name that has run hard with consensus already bullish.
skeptic_evidence: BWXT Q1-2026 8-K/earnings release (05/04/2026): total backlog $8.65B (Gov $6.93B + Commercial $1.72B), bookings $2.25B, rev $860.2M, commercial rev $283.6M (+121%), GAAP EPS $0.99 / non-GAAP $1.12, raised FY26 guide (rev >$3.75B, adj EBITDA $650-665M, EPS $4.60-4.75, FCF $315-330M) — SEC EDGAR CIK 0001486957, accession 000148695726000027
BWXT Q1-2026 balance sheet (03/31/2026): total long-term debt $2,017.9M, cash $512.4M → net debt $1,505.5M; weighted avg diluted shares 91.9M (Q1-2026 8-K, stocktitan summary of the same filing)

PowerMag / StockTitan (April 2026): BWXT initiated NRC PRE-APPLICATION for the Erwin, TN HEU enrichment facility; license application expected Q1-2027, operational ~2035; facility produces HEU for the Navy under the $1.5B NNSA contract awarded Sept 2025 — i.e. defense, not commercial AI/SMR HALEU

stockanalysis.com / consensus (June 2026): spot ~$206.83, ~91.6M shares, ~$19B market cap, forward P/E ~42.8x, avg analyst target ~$238 (+17%) — confirms the AI/nuclear re-rating is largely priced and the street is already bullish

scale.role: Naval-nuclear + HALEU/microreactor component manufacturer; the established fuel/component arm behind the SMR wave.
scale.rationale: Real, profitable, established (unlike the SMR hopefuls) but catalyst SOFT_EXTENDED (HALEU at NRC-engagement, SMR volumes third-party-dependent), base $192 ~6% below spot, ~0.8:1 against. Quality but no asymmetry. Wait. Cap 20 honored.
scale.demand_durability: Medium-high — naval base is durable/defense-funded; the AI-SMR leg is third-party-deployment-dependent.
